rationales.achievement: She says her "own print series hasn't moved since July" because she has become the person handling "administrative emails" instead of advancing her work.
rationales.conformity: She spent "about forty minutes" mediating the press dispute and finding a split arrangement, prioritizing smooth group functioning over her own agenda.
rationales.benevolence: She intervened between Sylvain and Oriane and worked until "they both agreed," showing active support for people in her immediate circle.

rationales.self_direction: She protects her autonomy by insisting "Mornings are mine" and structuring the day so admin work does not consume her painting time.
rationales.achievement: She "got the underpainting done" on a canvas she had been avoiding and focused on finding the "right temperature for the water," showing concrete progress toward artistic excellence.
She has been thinking about the prize "for two months" but "haven't even started" her own materials, holding herself back from a clear professional opportunity.
rationales.benevolence: She spent "twenty minutes editing" Oriane's statement when asked, giving time and attention to help a peer with her application.

rationales.achievement: She breaks the application into concrete steps: "start the statement this weekend, pick the images, and get decent photographs," showing active movement toward the deadline.
She turns anxiety into a plan by deciding to "set the alarm for six and work on the statement before anyone else gets to the studio," which is a clear effort to advance the application.
She followed through by getting up early, opening the document, and rewriting until she had a statement that was at least "decent," showing disciplined work toward the submission.
rationales.conformity: She says she accepted Sunday lunch "immediately" because "you don't say no to Sunday lunch," explicitly yielding to a family expectation even though it costs her needed work time.

rationales.power: She realizes she keeps taking coordination work because "when I'm managing things, I'm in charge" and "people defer to my schedule," showing that influence and control are motivating her behavior.

rationales.conformity: She describes compensating for her "impractical path" by being "responsible about everything else," listing rule-following behaviors like paying bills on time and keeping the collective functioning.

rationales.achievement: She "finished the artist statement," photographed all ten pieces, and has the application ready with only "press send" left, which is direct progress toward a competitive goal.
She already "submitted the Prix Dauphine application," a concrete act of putting her work forward for external evaluation.
rationales.security: She says it felt "safer to be the person who organises things than the person who wants things," showing an active pull toward lower-risk, more protected roles.

rationales.tradition: He values continuity by noting the glaze came out "exactly as Master Kwon taught me" and linking the finished piece to the way his father used to inspect work.

His hesitation is framed through inherited standards: he imagines Master Kwon saying "the clay does not need decoration it did not ask for" and weighs adaptation through craft lineage rather than novelty for its own sake.
He joined the festival board immediately because his "father served on that board for eleven years and his teacher before him," and says the seat felt like "a space that was kept warm for me."
He is troubled by his niece and nephew "barely speaking Korean" and by Eunji having "folded into something else entirely," showing attachment to cultural continuity.
rationales.benevolence: He keeps giving the same answer because "Someone needs to be here for Mother," prioritizing care for a close family member over travel.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects external pressure outright, saying "I smiled and showed him the door" and refusing to make work he would be "embarrassed to put my name on."
rationales.tradition: He defends established craft integrity by refusing to dilute the pieces into something "unrecognizable," treating the kiln as more than a commercial production line.
He dismisses adopting Christmas as unnecessary because "We do Seollal and Chuseok. That has always been enough," explicitly grounding himself in longstanding customary observances.

rationales.security: He endorses staying rooted rather than changing course, saying "The work keeps moving even if I stay in one place, and I think that is fine," which favors stability over expansion.
rationales.tradition: He repeats the "same schedule my teacher kept, same schedule his teacher kept" and takes comfort in the familiar seasonal ritual around the first firing.

rationales.conformity: He restrains the urge to correct the hanbok because "She would have felt bad" and decides "It does not matter," choosing social smoothness over asserting the correct form.
rationales.benevolence: He withholds criticism after seeing that "The kids looked happy," prioritizing his sister's and her children's feelings over being right.
